attribution.pr: AI-assisted: requires human review before merging

hooks.command: [ "$stop_hook_active" = "1" ] && exit 0; cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" || exit 0; [TEST_GUARD]OUT=$([TEST_COMMAND] 2>&1); CODE=$?; [ "$CODE" -ne 0 ] && { echo "$OUT" | tail -10 >&2; echo '{"decision": "block", "reason": "Tests must pass before completing. Fix failing tests first."}'; }; exit 0

hooks.prompt: Review the code changes in this session. Check for: (1) hardcoded secrets or credentials, (2) missing input validation on API endpoints, (3) SQL injection or command injection patterns, (4) missing auth checks. If critical issues found, return {"decision": "block", "reason": "..."}. If clean, return {"ok": true}.

hooks.command: IN=$(cat 2>/dev/null); [ "$(printf '%s' "$IN" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active // false' 2>/dev/null)" = "true" ] && exit 0; CWD=$(printf '%s' "$IN" | jq -r '.cwd // empty' 2>/dev/null); { [ -n "$CWD" ] && cd "$CWD" 2>/dev/null; } || cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null || exit 0; git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; CRE='sk_live_[a-zA-Z0-9]{10,}|api_key.*=.*[a-zA-Z0-9]{10,}|password.*=.*[a-zA-Z0-9]{8,}|token.*=.*[a-zA-Z0-9]{10,}'; DEF=$(git symbolic-ref -q --short refs/remotes/origin/HEAD 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$DEF" ] && for c in origin/main origin/master main master; do git rev-parse -q --verify "$c" >/dev/null 2>&1 && { DEF=$c; break; }; done; RANGE=""; [ -n "$DEF" ] && git rev-parse -q --verify "$DEF" >/dev/null 2>&1 && RANGE="$DEF...HEAD"; DIFF=$(git diff HEAD 2>/dev/null; [ -n "$RANGE" ] && git diff "$RANGE" 2>/dev/null); HIT=$(printf '%s\n' "$DIFF" | grep -aE '^\+[^+]' | grep -aiE "$CRE" | grep -avEi '[.][.][.]|example|placeholder|xxxx|your_|<[a-z]|changeme|dummy|redacted' | head -1); if [ -n "$HIT" ]; then jq -nc --arg r "Closure blocked: credential-like pattern in the block diff (same check as context-review C1). Review and remove before completing." '{decision:"block",reason:$r}'; exit 0; fi; LIM=5242880; FILES=$(git diff HEAD --name-only 2>/dev/null; [ -n "$RANGE" ] && git diff "$RANGE" --name-only 2>/dev/null); BIG=$(printf '%s\n' "$FILES" | sort -u | while read -r f; do sz=""; [ -n "$f" ] && [ -f "$f" ] && sz=$(wc -c <"$f" 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$sz" ] && [ "$sz" -gt "$LIM" ] && echo "$f"; done | head -1); if [ -n "$BIG" ]; then jq -nc --arg r "Closure blocked: a file added in this block exceeds the 5MB size cap ($BIG). Confirm this belongs in the repo before completing." '{decision:"block",reason:$r}'; exit 0; fi; exit 0

echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | jq -r '.command // ""' | grep -qE '(rm -rf /|DROP DATABASE|format C:|del /f /s /q C:)' && echo '{"decision": "block", "reason": "Potentially destructive command blocked. Confirm manually if intentional."}' || true

jq -nc --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" --arg tool "$CLAUDE_TOOL_NAME" --arg input "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" --arg session "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID" '{timestamp: $ts, session: $session, tool: $tool, input: ($input | .[:300])}' >> ~/.claude/audit/$(basename $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR).jsonl 2>/dev/null || true

LAST=$(cat "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/session/last-arch-audit" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); DONE=$(grep -cE '^\| *[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} *\|' "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/implementation-checklist.md" 2>/dev/null); DONE=${DONE:-0}; NOW=$(date +%s); DIFF=$((NOW - LAST)); if [ "$DONE" -ge 1 ] && [ $DIFF -gt 604800 ]; then DAYS=$((DIFF / 86400)); LAST_DATE=$([ $LAST -eq 0 ] && echo 'never' || date -r $LAST '+%Y-%m-%d' 2>/dev/null || date -d "@$LAST" '+%Y-%m-%d' 2>/dev/null || echo 'unknown'); echo "⚠️  Arch audit overdue — last run: $LAST_DATE (${DAYS}d ago). Run /arch-audit to check compliance with latest Anthropic docs."; fi

BLK=$(ls -t "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/session/"block-*.md 2>/dev/null | head -1); if [ -n "$BLK" ]; then MT=$(stat -f %m "$BLK" 2>/dev/null || stat -c %Y "$BLK" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); NOW=$(date +%s); AGE=$(( (NOW - MT) / 86400 )); if [ "$AGE" -ge 5 ]; then NAME=$(basename "$BLK" .md | sed 's/^block-//'); echo "⚠️  Block '$NAME' has been open ${AGE}d. Still in scope? Consider closing it (Phase 8) or splitting the work."; fi; fi
[ -f .claude/CLAUDE.local.md ] && echo '⚠️  Active .claude/CLAUDE.local.md overrides in effect — re-read to restore session context.' || true
